review_title: The best ELT tool on the market
description: Matillion is the single most important piece of software I use on a day-to-day basis. Its' ability to blend a ton of out-of-the-box functionality with the ability to script custom components in Python to author our own integrations is invaluable. It has enabled us to build transformed data within our warehouse, use pre-authored integrations to pull data from vendors, write our own integrations to pull from vendors, and is the single tool we use along with Redshift to provide visibility to all of the data within our business. I am a huge fan of Matillion and have used it in 2 different orgs. It is one of the only software tools I will champion across any org I move to.
author: James M.
pros: -Extremely easy deployment with cloud formation template. -Excellent pre-authored integrations that are easily extensible/customizable. -Easy to use UI to build complex transformations. -Extremely flexible logic with static and grid variables to intelligently execute jobs and branch as needed.
cons: -No automated scheduling based on dependent table updates. (This is possible to set up, but not out of the box.) -Support SLAs have gotten worse over time.

review_title: As an ETL it is robust
description: It's not a bad product, but our team decided to portion the ETL function to an Azure-based service as they couldn't tie Matillion to our business continuity plan due to a lack of skills with this product.
author: Dan H.
pros: There is a vast number of options and data connectors on offer.
cons: When communicating with the support and billing teams, they are never around to answer. Phone support is useless as no one answers, and it takes a few days to get an answer via email. Took for ever to switch billing from credit card to invoicing.

description: I really like Matillion ETL but I was a bit disappointed with some of its limitations and quirks, like when Twitter updated his API all my jobs stopped working and the only thing I could do was develop everything again without using the Matillion component. When I was trying to make upsert procedures on RDS Databases, I was thrilled that the Matillion component has that option, bit it simply didn't work. It seems like these functionalities haven't got the same attention of the others, I contacted the Matillion support which is simply great and these things are still going to be fixed. When I wanted specific libraries of Python, I couldn't get it working on my instance because pip was out of date and it couldn't be updated. I tried to update and I messed up with my instance. Overall, Matillion is great, when you're creating a flow of data migration and you need to parse the data before and do some automations, but there are some cases when you need something a little more specific, that's when it becomes a pain.
author: Marcus Vinícius S.
pros: The ease to use, Matillion has a great set of tools that make complex and difficult process more simple and fast to develop.
cons: There are many tools in Matillion that doesn't have enough attention and because of that these tools are fragile, while some of them do not work.

review_title: Matillion - ELT process made easy , perfect integration tool for snowflake users !
description: The overall experience with Matillion is good , if they compared the product with other products feature may be they could become even more better .
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Matillion is a cloud based ELT tool and it is very easy to use . The data transfer rates are incredibly good since the product locates in cloud infrastructure and the infrastructure can be scaled up when required.
cons: The connectors to other data sources are limited when compare to what other rival product are offering . The job monitoring functionality is limited , the scheduling and job log features could have been improved. The GitHub integration could have been straight forward.

review_title: Easy to use solution, allowing rapid delivery of ELT processes
description: We came to Matillion while looking for a tool that would allow us to orchestrate our load processes for Amazon Redshift. We'd used other ETL tools previously, were blown away by how easy it was to pick up and understand Matillion. We liked the simplicity, and the fact that we could always dig into the SQL that was generated. New features are added frequently, and we usually find one or two improvements that directly impact us with each release. Their support team are also very responsive, and Matillion are always keen to help you get the most out of their software, so there's a lot of support if you want it. 2+ years later, and we're using Matillion to run all our ELT jobs across various data warehouse projects, and very happy with it.
author: Graham L.
pros: - Very intuitive design environment - super quick to learn. - Components map closely to SQL capabilities in Redshift. - Easy to view the SQL generated. - Sample, row count, and data lineage make it easy to unit test as you build. - Powerful management options via REST API (orchestration, Github integration, etc.). - Native AWS integration. - Aggressive release cycle, constantly adding new features. - Easy to document in-workflow. - Lots of third party integrations.
cons: - User concurrency/licencing model (simple, but can't be customised to fit your need). - Exception handling could be improved. - A single Task View would be helpful for multi-environment/project support.

review_title: Great for pipelining data to warehouse/lake, not getting it back
description: It was easy to get set up, and it worked great for pushing data into Snowflake as a data warehouse. However, we quickly realized that we would need yet another tool to get the data back out to the source systems to synchronize/integrate it. We could sort of do it with Matillion, but it required a lot of custom programming and was not very intuitive. We ended up using a different iPaaS tool that could handle traffic/integration in both directions.
author: Michael S.
pros: It is extremely fast and easy to pull data from various sources and pipe it into Snowflake. The graphic interface lets this happen without programming.
cons: There is almost no ability to get data back from your warehouse into the other systems to synchronize the data. It's great to have it all in the warehouse, but it seems pretty critical to have that data flowing back to other systems that are part of your environment. The pricing model is frustrating. You are billed for when the machine instance is ON, not when it is actively doing something. So, if you have a couple hours of job that run in a day, you have to shut down the machine to save money the rest of the day. With it shut down, development can't happen. So, we had to turn it on and off all the time. It would be much better if they billed as it is used, not as it is on.
author_profession: Director, Business Intelligence and Web Services

review_title: Just started using Matillion this week
description: Overall experience with Matillion has been great. The first call with [SENSITIVE CONTENT] set the tone of who and what Matillion can offer our company. [SENSITIVE CONTENT] was kind, not pushy, relatable, and truly tried to understand our use case. [SENSITIVE CONTENT] really sold us on proceeding with a demo after sharing some of the functionality of the MDL tool available for free in AWS marketplace. While we very much could simply use the free tool, [SENSITIVE CONTENT] helped us understand why we should consider the full product. Finally, [SENSITIVE CONTENT] was a great account rep to work with. Before our first call he had looked up our company and understood our business (which is not easy to do). We are in a sensitive are of health care and [SENSITIVE CONTENT] opened up and shared a personal story relating to the line of work we are in. Other companies/products we looked into and has calls with did not take the time to understand our business or empathize with our customers. The personal touch from the above folks was a nice addition to the great product Matillion offers.
author: John D.
pros: Matillion is incredibly easy to deploy in AWS. In addition, the security and compliance aspects are all handled on the customer side due to the deployment method. The UI is easy to use and the price point was the most competitive.
cons: We haven't used it enough to encounter negative aspects yet but having to pay for the full amount of time the VM is up is going to be cost prohibitive for our small team.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Satisfied!
author: Alexander H.
pros: The collection of components; especially the integration components that hook into a very popular CRM and another quite popular ERP.
cons: The python component. Depending on the box where your instance is hosted, this component will drag along like a broken train or move at the speed of shooting star.

description: Matillion is a great out of the box product with minimal requirements. You spin the machine up, allow your database's firewall to communicate with Matillion. Start creating jobs, schedule them, and sit back. It helps you focus on visualizing your data.
author: Erick B.
pros: It is a pay for what you use type of software. Most other ETL platforms are a flat monthly fee, sometimes at crazy prices. The pay for what you use model makes it easy to justify cost to leadership. You can schedule jobs to make sure you maximize your time.
cons: The user experience feels a little dated. Some things could be better displayed/ explained.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: We are using it to do the extract load and transfer for our data warehouse
author: Ravi V.
pros: - Quick to start - Consumption based model - Works well with Snowflake
cons: - Harder to find resources with skills - Cost relatively higher

description: I like working on matillion , but this tool can be made more flexible by providing some of the additional features.
author: KOTESWAR RAO K.
pros: 1) Most of the components are user friendly. 2) Development of ETL orchestrations and transformation consumes less time. 3) Advanced features are available in some of the components makes the complex scenarios achievable.
cons: 1) OAuth document does not provide the details of proper permissions and access levels on account_id or client_id which are required for a connector. 2) Differentiation of naming convention of metrics between the console and matillion data model , mapping document of these naming conventions is not available. 3) Connector for Outbrain is not available. 4) Indirect file loading concept is not available , for example if we have five files with same structure , reg ex can not be used to load all files in a single component(s3 load,s3 put or excel) and need to use the file iterator. 5) Loss of properties when the component configuration is changed from Basic to Advanced features , ideally the component should include all the properties of basic and then additional setting should be provided.

review_title: Matillion is an Easy To Leverage (ETL) solution!
author: Francis T.
pros: - Easy to orchestrate an ETL pipeline - Integration with Redshift and other AWS services provides convenience - Support team is easy to reach and have relatively quick response times - They also support the other 2 popular cloud data warehouses, Snowflake and BigQuery - Some components are available out to the box for easy integration - Task history is useful for debugging and monitoring current state of ETL pipelines - A GUI is provided for implementing complicated workflows, that can be easily followed
cons: - Since it's mainly GUI based, it becomes difficult for data engineers to compare changes between old and new versions of an ETL. - There are hard limitations for the number of concurrent users. This is part of the pricing model which is the reason. - Integration with GIT is not yet available but we heard that it would be coming in the near future.

author: David W.
pros: Matillion is very intuitive. The GUI is easy to use and fairly easy to learn. The support team is great and they respond to your questions quickly. I like how the Matillion team is frequently looking to add new functionality and features. The new keyword search functionality is very helpful.
cons: Matillion works great extracting data from a variety of databases and is very efficient unloading onto S3 or Amazon Redshift. However, when we attempted to use it to unload large datasets to Microsoft SQL Server it was very slow and we experienced performance issues.

description: An easy to use tool that has allowed me to build a fully automated data warehouse to report to the business, it was easy to learn and implement, and as difficult as SQL to master. I think the real complexity comes from optimising complex workflows, the more T you need in the ETL/ELT process, the more Matillion falls down. This being said it is a very good tool for 90% of your work.
author: Joe E.
pros: It is easy to use and quick to get started, there are many inbuilt APIs and transformations that integrate it across a broad suite of systems, and enable you to transform data for the business without having to spend too much time thinking about scripts or bespoke batch files.
cons: The filter transformer is pretty minimal considering the power of the SQL it is utilising, it cannot use compound logic or reference other columns/objects in the query. There is also optimisation issues in very complex workspaces. Both of these have easy to use workarounds but it is unfortunate it is not supported more at surface level.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: The experience is very positive at the beginning but as the project grows and there are more and more workloads and data being processed Matillion starts to become a bottleneck.
author: Maksymilian M.
pros: It is very easy to implement features and changes to existing workload. It is very good for creating POCs
cons: It is not scalable and has numerous limitations. Concurrency setting for Matillion limits the possibility of using it for multiple warehouses. It is highly insufficient when executing commands parallel and does not utilise the resources to it's full potential. The environment limitation is also very problematic when trying to scale.

review_title: Have been using Matillion for past 9 months
description: We have developed Data Warehouse for our business using Matillion. We employ 6k+ people, and Matillion was a perfect match for our needs. Storing data in Google Big query allows seamless access to it from other Cloud Products I.E. Google Data Studio. I love the product, and limitless possibilities it gives us. Pulling the data from anything you may think of, transforming it and then outputting into Big Query is very easy and straight forward.
author: Paul C.
pros: My background is development and I love the simplicity of the Matillion, you can very easy transform your thought process into blocks in Matillion, and debug any issues at a very granular level.
cons: Matillion crashes from time to time, meaning you need to log back on to the system, but its benefits definitely outweigh this small issue.

review_title: Matillion for BigQuery - United HealthGroup Data Engineer Review
description: Very useful tool. If you're looking for a tool for your analytics data warehouse which is fast, convenient and easy to learn, then this is the tool for you
author: Ryan Allen A.
pros: Its very easy to learn. The UI is great and easy to navigate. It utilized the ELT approach which makes the data integration fast. Security and access control is well implemented and there are a lot of components to use.
cons: Its a one way ETL, I'm using the BigQuery product and all the destination are in BigQuery, it would also be nice if we can load data to the sources.

description: It's been great. The support team has been very responsive in resolving some Azure-related issues, tuning our instance, and helping to understand the nuances of how it works. It's been a significant reduction in development time since we migrated to Matillion.
author: Brian R.
pros: Simple, intuitive interface. I replaced a complicated series of scripts, "ETL" products, and Python code with a simple-to-understand Matillion instance.
cons: We use it on Snowflake for Azure, so there are several features that have not been migrated from the AWS version, yet. An option for a larger server by default in the Azure Marketplace.

description: Its helping us build a data warehouse for visualizing debt and customer segmentation in our business. It means we are getting real insight into our business. Thank you!
author: Roger E.
pros: User interface and range of connections. Powerful capabilities. Compatibility with AWS
cons: Licensing is not flexible, Why cant you specify the number of concurrent users for a given instance? It comes with a fixed amount, if you don't like it, update to a much large and expensive instance that we do not need.

review_title: Solution that works fine for almost all applications
description: If you really use your data and work on a daily basis with your data warehouse you should consider using Matillion. We had no downtimes and the service is brilliant. We connected all of our data sources and it works really well. But it got its price. Compared to other solutions the pricing is good for growing businesses.
author: Christoph L.
pros: Connectivity Really good customer service Individual solutions if necessary No downtimes Hosted on Amazon
cons: Pretty expensive A bit hard to set up initially

author: Levan S.
pros: * It's very easy to start building scalable production ready ETL pipeline. * GUI is very intuitive. * Has wide variety of integrations available. *
cons: * Error handling/debugging is on a poor level. * No good way to version queries/scripts or jobs. * Not really suitable if you need to build something very complex, as it's not very customizable.

author: Ellis C.
pros: The features and ease of use are good. The transformations act as a documentation for the self because of the readability.
cons: The customer service is very poor. When they eventually respond to messages it is with an unhelpful response. Generally they will blame us for issues on their end, or they misunderstand the problem completely. It's not what we need from a tool The paid training we took was also pretty basic

description: We are able to quickly execute on ongoing ETL projects and ad hoc needs.
author: Spencer B.
pros: Ease of use and breadth of functionality.
cons: We had a billing issue at one point when trying to renew but that was mostly due to AWS Marketplace and not Matillion as a product.

description: Class leading, can turn a new file around in hours and days.
author: Christopher D.
pros: Pretty straightforward compared to other ETL tools, traditional engineers pick up in no time at all.
cons: The google version is not as rich as the AWS one

review_title: A Great ELT and warehousing toolset in a clean GUI
description: We're pulling multiple datasets together and integrating them in star schemas with Matillion, as well as doing sophisticated data analysis.
author: David L.
pros: The GUI and design makes it easy to not only functionalize your ELTs, but also show a graphical DAG to stakeholders.
cons: I wish it had an out-of-the-box API gateway to receive webhook requests from third-party data sources.
